lecture_context.lecture_title: What We See and What We Value: AI with the Human Perspective

unit.name: Embodied AI and open-world robot planning
unit.description: Introduces robotics as the closure of the perception-action loop and describes a method that uses LLMs and VLMs for open-ended robot control.
unit.summary: Fei-Fei Li frames embodied AI as the point where vision connects to action, likening robotics to the evolutionary jump from eyes to movement while cautioning that current robots remain slow, clumsy, and poor at generalization [ts=2871s] [ts=2911s]. She then presents an open-world manipulation system that accepts natural-language instructions like opening a drawer while avoiding a vase, using an LLM to generate structured instructions and a VLM to detect referenced objects and update motion-planning heat maps [ts=2990s] [ts=3150s]. The segment stresses that this approach reduces reliance on closed-world robot training and demonstrates diverse tasks such as articulated-object manipulation, table setting, and sweeping [ts=3084s] [ts=3213s].

key_points.text: Embodied AI closes the loop between perception and action.
Current robots are still slow, clumsy, and weak at generalizable real-world adaptation.
The goal is open-world robot generalization from open-ended instructions rather than a predesignated closed task set.
An LLM converts task instructions into code-like steps and a VLM grounds referenced objects in the scene.
Motion-planning heat maps encode where the robot should go and what it should avoid, such as a vase.

kp_catalog.name: Perception-action integration
kp_catalog.description: Robots should sense only what is necessary for the current task and continuously couple perception to action rather than fully reconstructing the world at all times. Robotics is framed as the closure of the loop between seeing and doing, requiring both perception and action planning.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because perception-action integration clarifies robots should sense only what is necessary for the current task and continuously couple perception to action rather than fully reconstructing the world at all times, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices though roles split between capstone and supporting.

kp_catalog.name: Dynamic multi-actor activity understanding
kp_catalog.description: Understanding who is doing what and what happens next in videos remains an open problem beyond static scene recognition.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because dynamic multi-actor activity understanding synthesizes understanding who is doing what and what happens next in videos remains an open problem beyond static scene recognition into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.

kp_catalog.name: Image captioning and multimodal scene description
kp_catalog.description: Vision systems can map images to natural language descriptions, first with CNN+LSTM pipelines and later with multimodal LLMs.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because image captioning and multimodal scene description clarifies vision systems can map images to natural language descriptions, first with CNN+LSTM pipelines and later with multimodal LLMs, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
